page: Amazon Web Services m8gd.4xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by custom AWS Graviton processors. Built on the ARMv9 architecture, the server features 16 dedicated physical cores utilizing the Neoverse-V2 model running at 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance includes 950 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong capabilities in memory and data processing. The server achieves a top-tier Passmark memory score, supported by strong uncached memory bandwidth speeds of 296.82 GB/sec for read operations and 133.57 GB/sec for write operations. Single-threaded gzip compression and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression both register strong performance profiles. While Geekbench multi-core and single-core scores are strong, medium-model LLM prompt processing is relatively weak at 30.79 tokens/sec, though text generation remains average at 13.21 tokens/sec.

The ARM-based architecture delivers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency compared to traditional x86 hardware classes, especially for workloads that benefit from dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading. The inclusion of 950 GB of fast local NVMe SSD storage makes this instance highly suitable for database operations, web serving, and general-purpose applications requiring high-speed local caching. It is well-suited for database hosting, static web serving, and multi-threaded data decompression tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gd.4xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by a 16-core AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 64.0 GB of RAM, 950 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this instance provides dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading. Performance benchmarks show top-tier memory scores, with strong uncached read and write memory bandwidth alongside strong multi-threaded decompression capabilities. However, medium-model LLM prompt processing remains a relative bottleneck. Given its balanced resource allocation and fast local storage, the m8gd.4xlarge offers high qualitative cost efficiency for database operations, static web serving, and general-purpose workloads requiring high-speed local caching.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gd.4xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose server featuring 16 dedicated Graviton cores, 64 GB RAM, and 950 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier memory performance for databases.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8gd.4xlarge is a 16-core ARM64 server with 64 GB RAM and 950 GB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for general-purpose and database workloads.
tagline: An ARM-powered general-purpose server featuring dedicated physical cores and high-speed local NVMe SSD storage for balanced, data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory paired with 950 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier memory performance and strong uncached memory read/write bandwidth
8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed by the Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for database operations, static web serving, and data decompression
